title: Deceptive
wrong: Induces or exploits a false belief about a material fact.
example: Hidden odds; disguised ads; value obfuscation.

title: Manipulative
wrong: Bypasses or subverts rational agency via bias, emotion, or hidden influence.

title: Coercive
wrong: Removes, withholds, or penalises acceptable alternatives; applies pressure.
example: Forced action; obstructed exit; withholding progression to compel pay; play-by-appointment.

title: Exploitative
wrong: Takes unfair advantage of an asymmetry or vulnerability for the provider's gain — even if the user is not deceived.
example: Loot boxes; whale-targeting; engineered grind; SES-targeted monetisation.

title: Malicious
wrong: Design deliberately built or deployed to work against the user's interest.
example: Patented spend-maximisation; covert data capture; 'malicious interface design'.
